decks.notes: The main idea of this deck is to quickly assemble a board presence of artifact-based creatures and utilize cards like Urza's Bauble, Mishra's Bauble, and Thoughtcast to draw additional cards and maintain card advantage. Emry, Lurker of the Loch acts as both a source of card advantage and recurring threat. The deck also utilizes disruption with cards like Force of Will and Boomerang Basics to disrupt opponents' game plans. The inclusion of powerful artifacts like Mox Opal and The Fantasticar helps generate mana and close out games quickly.

The deck revolves around assembling the combo piece, The Fantasticar, to create a powerful engine for card draw and mana generation. This enables the player to dig through their deck quickly, disrupt the opponent's game plan with discard spells like Thoughtseize, and protect their combo with counter spells like Force of Will. The deck also has a mix of aggressive threats like Murktide Regent and Barrowgoyf to apply pressure on the opponent while setting up the combo. Overall, it is a versatile and adaptable strategy that aims to win the game through synergy and efficiency.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize The Fantasticar as a key combo piece, alongside other spells like Stormchaser's Talent and Flow State, to generate mana and card advantage while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will. The deck aims to manipulate the board state through bounce effects and control spells, allowing the pilot to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent. The presence of cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal enables explosive plays and fast wins, making it a formidable combo deck in the Legacy format.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize a combination of fast mana sources and discard spells to quickly assemble the key pieces of "The Fantasticar", a powerful combo card. By generating mana quickly and disrupting the opponent's hand, the deck aims to bring out "The Fantasticar" as soon as possible and combo off with cards like Boggart Trawler and Fell the Profane to secure the win. The deck also utilizes artifacts such as Mox Opal and Lotus Petal to further accelerate its game plan.

The main focus of this deck is to apply early pressure on the opponent while controlling the game with disruptive spells like Force of Will and Thoughtseize. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Daze and Fatal Push, while also maintaining card advantage through efficient card draw with cards like Brainstorm and Ponder. The inclusion of creatures like Murktide Regent and Nethergoyf help to close out the game quickly, while Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides additional card advantage. The mana base is streamlined to support the aggressive and controlling game plan effectively.

This Mardu Aggro deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's hand with Thoughtseize and Cabal Therapy while applying pressure with aggressive creatures like Amped Raptor, Guide of Souls, and Ocelot Pride. The deck also includes efficient removal with Swords to Plowshares and combines creature sacrifice synergy with Goblin Bombardment. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory provide additional ways to close out the game quickly, while utilizing dual lands like Plateau, Karakas, and Scrubland ensures consistency in mana base. The deck also includes Wasteland for additional disruption and resource denial.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying threats while disrupting the opponent's game plan. By efficiently utilizing tempo spells like Daze and Force of Will, as well as disruptive land destruction with Wasteland, the deck aims to keep the opponent off balance while continually applying pressure. Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent provide powerful threats that can end the game quickly if left unchecked. With a mix of card draw, removal, and counterspells, this deck aims to control the pace of the game and pivot to a winning position.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's actions and resources through counter spells, card draw, and removal spells. It aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building up its own resources with card advantage and selection. Key cards such as Teferi, Time Raveler and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd provide additional control options and protection for the player's board. The deck also utilizes a land destruction package with Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base. Overall, the main strategy revolves around denying the opponent's game plan while establishing a strong board presence.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly generate mana and cast multiple spells in a single turn to build up storm count, utilizing cards like Rite of Flame, Seething Song, and Desperate Ritual. By using cards like Ruby Medallion to reduce the cost of spells, the deck aims to chain together spells and achieve a high storm count. Burning Wish allows for access to additional cards from the sideboard, providing flexibility and options for different scenarios. Ultimately, the goal is to create a large storm count and finish off the opponent with a powerful spell like Ral, Monsoon Mage or Storm of Memories.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by using counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive cards like Stifle. The key concept is to manage the opponent's threats with efficient answers while advancing your own game plan with card advantage engines like Tamiyo and Phelia. Utilizing powerful planeswalkers and versatile spells like Brainstorm and Swords to Plowshares, this deck aims to outlast and outvalue the opponent, ultimately coming out on top through strategic decision-making and manipulation of the game state.

The main concept of this Bant Control deck is to utilize a combination of powerful control elements such as counterspells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce, removal like Swords to Plowshares, and hand disruption like Thoughtseize to maintain control over the game. The deck also utilizes card selection and card advantage tools like Brainstorm, Ponder, and Quantum Riddler to set up its strategy. Additionally, cards like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Triumph of Saint Katherine provide additional value and options for the player to outmaneuver their opponent and secure victory.

This deck is focused on generating a large amount of mana quickly through cards like Dark Ritual and Lion's Eye Diamond, and using that mana to cast multiple spells in a single turn to build up storm count. The goal is to assemble a combination of cards that will allow the player to cast Tendrils of Agony and drain their opponent's life total in one big burst. The deck also includes cards like Thoughtseize and Duress to disrupt the opponent's game plan and protect its own combo.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly establish a board presence with artifact creatures and disrupt the opponent's game plan with counterspells and disruptive spells. By using powerful artifacts like Chalice of the Void and Urza's Saga, the deck aims to control the board and win the game through efficient aggression. Additionally, the deck utilizes cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Mishra's Bauble to generate card advantage and improve consistency. The inclusion of cards like Force of Will and Force of Negation helps in protecting our key pieces and ensuring our game plan remains intact.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to generate enough mana to cast powerful spells and win the game. By utilizing cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal, the deck aims to generate a large amount of mana early on to cast spells like The Fantasticar and other powerful spells. The deck also includes disruption elements like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to protect the combo and ensure its success. Additionally, cards like Brainstorm and Ponder help with card selection to find the necessary pieces for the combo.

The deck focuses on combining aggressive creatures with disruptive elements, utilizing cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Deafening Silence to slow down opponents while deploying threats like Stoneforge Mystic and Skyclave Apparition to apply pressure. Aether Vial aids in quickly getting creatures onto the battlefield, while Karakas and Wasteland disrupt opposing strategies. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional value and synergy within the deck. Ultimately, the goal is to control the game through disruption and aggressive creature presence.

The deck aims to control the pace of the game through counterspells and removal spells while utilizing card draw and manipulation to maintain card advantage. The creatures in the deck are either disruptive or provide additional card advantage, helping to control the board state and ultimately win the game through attrition. The mana base is designed to support the control elements of the deck, with a mix of dual lands and fetch lands to ensure consistent mana access.
